scope_item: Item 1 of 5 — HERO SWAP (highest priority within HNOTE)
target: build-scripts/generate-html.js — Overview tab template (lines ~1366-1389): replace four-up scorecard sc-hero with display-scale thesis sentence followed by compact letterspaced stat row. Add new CSS rules for .thesis-hero, .thesis-claim, .thesis-stat-row, .thesis-stat. Keep stat figures (counts.total, 0, dome+silent failures, Wrong Side) but demote them to body weight (14-15px) with small-cap labels. Verdict bar chart (line 1366) is moved to display AFTER the stat row so the thesis is the first visible content below the title-block. Accuracy-math-section (lines 1390-1402) is unchanged.
curmudgeon_review: pending — per operator HNOTE: route through curmudgeon for adversarial review BEFORE integration; do NOT auto-integrate
current_summary: Overview tab currently presents (1) title-block, (2) verdict bar chart, (3) four-up scorecard sc-hero with four large stat cards (Claimed Wins / Actual Wins / Actual Failures / Wrong Side), then (4) accuracy-math-section. The four-up tiles function as a dashboard at hero scale; the round-2 external design reviewer notes that the dashboard pitch presents four numbers without a coherent thesis sentence at display scale and recommends promoting the thesis to the hero position with stats demoted to support.
replacement_summary: Replace the four-up sc-hero scorecard with a thesis-hero block: a display-scale thesis sentence (clamp(2.0rem, 5vw, 3.4rem) renders ~38–54 CSS px, larger than the 2.4rem title-block h1) followed by a single compact stat row carrying the same four figures (counts.total catalog entries / 0 confirmed / dome+silent failures / Wrong-Side timestamp). The stat row uses ~14-15px body type with uppercase letterspaced labels (.thesis-stat-cap), preserving every link and arithmetic detail from the original sc-cards. Verdict bar chart relocates to the position immediately after the stat row (was before the sc-hero) so the reading order becomes Title → Thesis → Supporting figures → Verdict distribution → Accuracy math drill-down.
thesis_sentence_text: None of the ${counts.total} predictions produces a dome-specific result that standard physics cannot already explain.
thesis_sentence_authoring_note: Operator-authored per HNOTE; treat as final wording. The literal 69 is templated to ${counts.total} per the project-wide rule that all numerical counts in prose are computed from data at build time. counts.total resolves to 69 for ECM V51.1 (verified 2026-04-26 against live dome site); if the dome publishes a higher count, the thesis sentence updates automatically without any prose edit.

patch_segments.old_block_text:
${generateVerdictBarChart(tally, wins.length)}
${counts.total}
Claimed "Wins"
The author claims ${counts.total} confirmed predictions and zero falsifications
0
Actual Wins
Not one claim produces a result that the globe model can't explain and the dome uniquely can
${failures.dome_claimed_failures} + ${silentFailures}
Actual Failures
He admits ${failures.dome_claimed_failures}. We found ${silentFailures} more he silently removed. Details →
Wrong Side
Timestamp Error
His blockchain proof timestamps the observations, not the predictions. As of April 2026, the author concedes this distinction in writing — but hasn't fixed the underlying proof structure. Details →
patch_segments.new_block_text:
None of the ${counts.total} predictions produces a dome-specific result that standard physics cannot already explain.

${counts.total}
catalog entries
0
confirmed independent wins
${failures.dome_claimed_failures} + ${silentFailures}
acknowledged + silent failures
Wrong side
timestamp error
${generateVerdictBarChart(tally, wins.length)}
patch_segments.placement_notes:
New block placement: BETWEEN closing of title-block (line 1364) AND opening
(line 1390). The verdict-bar-chart line that was at line 1366 is relocated to AFTER the thesis-hero section, before the accuracy-math-section. This keeps the verdict bar on the overview (per HNOTE Item 2: do not pre-resolve, just preserve) but lets the thesis claim be the first visible content below the title-block, matching the round-2 design reviewer recommendation.

patch_segments.append_strategy: Append the rules to the existing single-line CSS string in generate-html.js right after the .stance-statement rule. The exact insertion point is the end of the .stance-statement{...} declaration so the new selectors slot into the same flat CSS block. No existing rule is modified.

acceptance_criteria: AC1: After integration, the rendered Overview tab shows, in order: title-block, thesis-hero (with thesis-claim and thesis-stat-row), generateVerdictBarChart output, accuracy-math-section. The four-up scorecard sc-hero block is removed from the Overview.

AC2: thesis-claim text reads exactly: "None of the 69 predictions produces a dome-specific result that standard physics cannot already explain." (where 69 is rendered from counts.total at build time).
AC3: thesis-claim renders larger than any title-block h1 at desktop widths >= 1100px (target: rendered font-size >= 2.8rem; 2.4rem for .title-block h1 — measured by getComputedStyle).

AC4: All four stat figures preserve their original numeric meaning: counts.total, 0, dome_claimed_failures + silentFailures, and the Wrong-Side timestamp callout. Each stat that previously linked to a tab/anchor still links there (failures → #p3-failures on wins tab; Wrong Side → #timestamp-error on timestamp tab).

AC5: thesis-claim plus thesis-stat-row both fit above the fold on a 1440x900 desktop viewport with the existing tab-bar present. Measured via Playwright bottom <= 900 - tab-bar-height - browser-chrome.
AC6: At <=520px viewport (mobile), thesis-claim wraps to <=24ch, font-size scales down via clamp, and thesis-stat-row collapses to single-column.
AC7: Verdict bar chart still renders on the Overview tab (not removed; only relocated from above the four-up to below the thesis-stat-row).
AC8: accuracy-math-section is unchanged — same DOM, same prose, same placement relative to the now-relocated verdict bar chart (still appears below the verdict bar chart on the Overview).
AC9: No changes to data/wins.json, data/sections.json, data/uncounted-failures.json, or any other data file. Only build-scripts/generate-html.js is modified.

AC10: WCAG 2.1 AA contrast: thesis-claim uses var(--heading) on var(--bg); thesis-stat-cap uses var(--ink-3) on var(--bg). Both must measure >= 4.5:1 in both light and dark themes; if --ink-3 falls short, fall back to var(--ink-2) per the EXP-211-redesign-v2 contrast ladder.
AC11: thesis-stat-row is keyboard-reachable — tab order traverses the two stat-cap anchors (failures and timestamp) in DOM order; focus ring matches site-wide :focus-visible style.

AC12: Print stylesheet (@media print) renders thesis-claim at a print-appropriate size; if existing print rules at line 644+ produce undersized output, add a print rule .thesis-claim{font-size:1.8rem;page-break-after:avoid}.

load_bearing_content_audit.purpose: Per HNOTE: "Verify the hero swap doesn't lose any load-bearing content from the original four-up stat tiles." This audit walks each old sc-card and confirms either preservation, demotion, or relocation.

cards.old_content: ${counts.total} | Claimed "Wins" | The author claims ${counts.total} confirmed predictions and zero falsifications
cards.new_treatment: Number ${counts.total} preserved as thesis-stat-num. Label "catalog entries" replaces "Claimed Wins" because thesis-claim already establishes that none of these are real wins; "catalog entries" is the neutral count of dome-listed items. The sub-label sentence ("author claims … and zero falsifications") is REMOVED here but is fully covered by accuracy-math-section directly below.
cards.rationale: The sub-label is editorial, not data. accuracy-math-section restates the zero-falsifications point with citations.

cards.old_content: 0 | Actual Wins | Not one claim produces a result that the globe model can't explain and the dome uniquely can
cards.new_treatment: Number 0 preserved as thesis-stat-num with thesis-stat-accent color treatment. Label "confirmed independent wins" sharpens "Actual Wins" (independence from globe-explanation is the actual criterion). Sub-label sentence is REMOVED — but it is the kernel of the new thesis-claim itself ("None of the 69 predictions produces a dome-specific result that standard physics cannot already explain"). So the sub-label content is PROMOTED to the thesis, not lost.
cards.rationale: This is the load-bearing claim. It is preserved AND promoted to display scale.

cards.old_content: ${failures.dome_claimed_failures} + ${silentFailures} | Actual Failures | He admits …. We found … more he silently removed. Details →
cards.new_treatment: Number formula preserved. Label "acknowledged + silent failures" makes the formula self-explanatory at a glance. The link to #p3-failures is preserved on the label text. The "He admits / We found / silently removed" sentence is REMOVED here but Section 3.1 (Verdict Summary Table) and Section 3.2-3.6 (per-verdict listings) carry the full breakdown one click away.
cards.rationale: Number AND link preserved. Editorial sub-label removed but content is one click away.

cards.old_content: Wrong Side | Timestamp Error | His blockchain proof timestamps the observations, not the predictions. As of April 2026, the author concedes this distinction in writing — but hasn't fixed the underlying proof structure. Details →
cards.new_treatment: Phrase "Wrong side" preserved as thesis-stat-num-words. Label "timestamp error" preserved. Link to #timestamp-error tab preserved on the label. The April-2026-concession-but-not-fixed sentence is REMOVED here but is fully present in the Latest Findings strip ("Dome Author Changes Timestamp Narrative — But Doesn't Fix the Problem") which appears further down on the same Overview tab AND in the dedicated Timestamp Error tab.
cards.rationale: Phrase, label, and link preserved. The status update is restated in Latest Findings on the same page.

load_bearing_content_audit.summary: No load-bearing content is lost. Every number, link, and primary phrase is preserved. The editorial sub-labels that explained each stat in 2-3 sentences are removed from the hero, but their content is either (a) absorbed into the new thesis-claim (sc-card #2), (b) one click away on a dedicated tab (sc-cards #3 and #4), or (c) restated within the same Overview tab in Latest Findings or accuracy-math-section.

hero_swap_verification.thesis_at_display_scale: Yes — clamp(2.0rem, 5vw, 3.4rem) renders at the 5vw breakpoint up to its 3.4rem ceiling. On a 1440px viewport, 5vw = 4.5rem, capped at 3.4rem (54.4 CSS px). On a 1100px viewport, 5vw = 3.4375rem, capped at 3.4rem. On 768px, 5vw = 2.4rem (close to 2.4rem floor of the title-block h1, still readable). All comfortably larger than .title-block h1 at 2.4rem on desktop sizes.
hero_swap_verification.stats_demoted_to_body_row: Yes — thesis-stat-num is 1.45rem (vs original 2.8rem), thesis-stat-cap is 0.74rem letterspaced uppercase. Total stat-row height ~3.5rem fits in a single row beneath the thesis.
hero_swap_verification.composition_above_the_fold: Confirmed via spec arithmetic — title-block top padding 3rem + h1 ~2.4rem + h1 ~1.6rem + 2x subtitle ~1.1rem + meta ~0.95rem + bottom padding 2rem + margin-bottom 2rem ≈ 14rem of title block. thesis-hero margin-top 1.5rem + claim ~3.4rem * 1.18 line-height + claim margin .9rem + stat-row margin-top 1.1rem + stat-row top padding .85rem + stat-row content ~3.5rem + bottom margin 1.75rem ≈ 13.5rem. Total above-fold height ~27.5rem ≈ 440 CSS px, well under 900px viewport even after subtracting tab-bar ~70px and browser chrome ~100px (leaves ~290px to spare).

alignment_to_round2_review.round2_recommendation_1_hero_swap: Round-1 F1, deferred to Phase C, now promoted to active by operator. Designer quote: "this single change does more for credibility than every body-text improvement combined." THIS EXP ADDRESSES IT.
alignment_to_round2_review.round2_recommendation_2_verdict_bar_audi: Out of scope for EXP-255. This is Item 2 of the HNOTE and a separate EXP. EXP-255 keeps the verdict bar on the Overview (relocates from before the four-up to after the thesis-stat-row); does NOT pre-resolve the audit.
alignment_to_round2_review.round2_recommendation_3_falsification_mo: Out of scope. Item 3, separate EXP.
alignment_to_round2_review.round2_recommendation_4_how_to_evaluate_: Out of scope. Item 4, separate EXP.
alignment_to_round2_review.round2_recommendation_5_eclipse_badge: Out of scope. Item 5, separate EXP.

anticipated_objections.objection: You hardcoded "None of the 69 predictions" but counts.total may not equal 69 in the future.
anticipated_objections.response: Templated to ${counts.total}. Current build resolves to 69 because that is the dome's claimed count for ECM V51.1 (verified 2026-04-26 against live https://john09289.github.io/predictions). If the dome publishes additional predictions, counts.total updates automatically and the thesis sentence updates with it. Our wins.json has 71 entries because two are sub-IDs for the dome's numbering collisions; counts.total filters to 3-digit IDs only via baseWins and reports the dome-claimed count of 69. This matches the existing convention used throughout the document.

anticipated_objections.objection: Removing the sub-label sentences from each sc-card loses load-bearing context.
anticipated_objections.response: See load_bearing_content_audit. Every numeric or linkable element is preserved. The 2-3-sentence sub-label prose is either absorbed into the thesis (sc-card #2 IS the thesis), restated below in accuracy-math-section (sc-card #1 zero-falsifications point), or carried in Latest Findings on the same tab (sc-card #4 April 2026 update). No load-bearing assertion vanishes.

anticipated_objections.objection: The dashboard pitch was deliberate per round-1 brief; the four numbers were the hero on purpose.
anticipated_objections.response: Round-1 R1 was the operator-and-reviewer-collaborative trade-off: dashboard pitch optimized for credibility-by-precision-numbers. Round-2 reviewer revisited and recommends thesis-at-display-scale because "the actual thesis is none of the 69 survives, and the thesis sentence belongs at display scale, not below the stats." Operator approved the swap explicitly in HNOTE-OPERATOR-DESIGN-REVIEW-V2-OVERVIEW-REWORK-001 Decision 1. The numbers stay; only their visual hierarchy changes.

anticipated_objections.objection: Verdict bar chart should not move; it was placed before the four-up deliberately.
anticipated_objections.response: Conservative call. The verdict bar chart was added by EXP-209 in Phase A1 to give a verdict-distribution view. EXP-255 moves it from "before the hero scorecard" to "after the thesis-stat-row" so the thesis-claim is the first visible content below the title. If curmudgeon thinks the verdict bar belongs above the stat row (so the reader sees the distribution before the supporting stats), the placement is a cheap CSS-order swap and can be revised in EXP-255-r2.

anticipated_objections.objection: thesis-claim with text-wrap:balance has uneven browser support.
anticipated_objections.response: text-wrap:balance is supported in Chromium 114+, Safari 17.4+, Firefox 121+ (all current at 2026-04). Browsers that ignore the property fall back to default text-wrap, which produces an acceptable but less-balanced line break. No content is lost; this is purely a visual polish that degrades gracefully.

anticipated_objections.objection: The dl/dt/dd structure for stat row is non-standard for visual layout.
anticipated_objections.response: dl/dt/dd is semantically correct for a name-value-pair list, which is exactly what a stat row is (each stat is a value with an associated label). It also gives screen readers the right grouping. The CSS uses display:grid on the dl and display:flex on its child wrappers; visually identical to a div-based layout, semantically richer.

anticipated_objections.objection: thesis-stat-num-words ("Wrong side") visually conflicts with the numeric thesis-stat-num style.
anticipated_objections.response: Addressed via .thesis-stat-num-words override (font-size:1.1rem, font-weight:700) which softens the size relative to the numeric figures so the row reads as "three numbers and one phrase" without the phrase looking visually broken. Curmudgeon should validate this at integration.

anticipated_objections.objection: Why max-width:64rem on .thesis-hero — is that arbitrary?
anticipated_objections.response: 64rem ≈ 1024px = the comfortable reading max for a centered hero on a 1440-1920px viewport. It approximately matches the visual width of the verdict bar chart on standard desktop widths so the eye flows from one to the next without a width jolt.

anticipated_objections.objection: thesis-stat-cap font-size 0.74rem is below the typical 0.85rem minimum body size.
anticipated_objections.response: 0.74rem (~11.8px) is appropriate for label/cap copy that is uppercase + letterspaced 0.08em (mirrors .ams-heading at 0.82rem letter-spacing 0.06em from EXP-211-redesign-v2). Per WCAG 2.1, no minimum font size is mandated; contrast and zoom are the binding requirements. Per AC10 the cap text uses var(--ink-3) over var(--bg) which is measured at 4.71:1 dark / 5.59:1 light per EXP-211-redesign-v2 contrast ladder — passes AA body 4.5:1.

kernel_of_truth: The four-up scorecard hero IS a clear, scannable summary of the review's core findings — that is what the round-1 design review approved and is why the operator initially chose it. The dashboard pitch communicates rigor and reduces apparent rhetoric. Round-2 reviewer is not saying the dashboard is bad; they are saying that BELOW a thesis sentence the dashboard becomes more powerful ("here is what we conclude; here are the supporting numbers") instead of presenting four numbers and asking the reader to infer the thesis. The hero swap preserves the dashboard data while installing the thesis as the entry point.
newsworthy: Critical-Review Overview gains thesis-first hero — dashboard demoted to support row beneath display-scale claim

prerequisites_satisfied.EXP_211_integrated: Yes — confirmed via tracker EXP-211.integrated=true at 2026-04-25T17:27:34Z. accuracy-math-section is in place at the position EXP-255 expects.
prerequisites_satisfied.EXP_209_verdict_bar_present: Yes — verdict bar chart is rendered by generateVerdictBarChart on line 1366 of build-scripts/generate-html.js.
prerequisites_satisfied.operator_authored_thesis_text: Yes — HNOTE provides the exact wording, treated as final.
prerequisites_satisfied.no_blocking_dependencies: EXP-255 does not depend on Items 2-5 of the same HNOTE, on EXP-190, or on the parallel 4.2.2/4.2.7 sections work (EXP-253 / EXP-254). Standalone.

routing_priority: high — Item 1 of the HNOTE is operator-stated highest priority within the rework
routing_note: Per HNOTE: "Substantive overview rework. Should route through curmudgeon for adversarial review (NOT auto-integrate)." Curmudgeon mission stated in HNOTE: verify (a) hero swap doesn't lose load-bearing content from old four-up; (d) visual hierarchy: thesis is hero, support is body, no competing elements; (e) DIRECTIVE-20260422-003 design rubric; (f) stress-test operator decision to remove four-up tiles from hero. Items (b) and (c) of the HNOTE curmudgeon mission relate to Items 3 and 4 — out of scope here.
out_of_scope: Item 2 (verdict bar audit + redundancy removal) — separate EXP.
Rewriting the thesis sentence content (operator-authored, treat as final).
Removing the stat numbers entirely (they stay as supporting figures).
Removing or restyling the verdict bar chart from EXP-209 (only relocating it).
Modifying any data file (data/wins.json, data/sections.json, data/uncounted-failures.json) — none required for this hero swap.
test_coverage_note: Existing test.js suite covers data/wins.json and data/sections.json integrity. EXP-255 modifies build-scripts/generate-html.js only; existing build-then-validate test (node build.js html → check exit code + DOM smoke test on docs/index.html) catches template syntax errors. After integration, recommend adding Playwright reachability tests for AC5 (above-fold) at 1440x900 and AC6 (mobile collapse) at 390x844, deferred from this EXP per the EXP-211-redesign-v2 precedent.
tldr_evidence: Round-2 external design reviewer says the Overview's hero needs a thesis sentence at display scale instead of four equal stat tiles. Operator approved the swap. EXP-255 replaces the four sc-cards with a single large thesis sentence followed by a compact letterspaced stat row carrying the same four figures.
tldr_verdict: Hero swap is a build-template-only change to build-scripts/generate-html.js with no data-file modifications. All numbers, links, and the load-bearing claim are preserved; the dashboard data is demoted to support, the thesis becomes the entry point. Routes through curmudgeon before integration.
